steps.title: Connect turnover to fading interest
steps.content: The passage discusses subscription cancellations and says sellers need incentives to bolster retention. That logic works if customers' initial enthusiasm quickly fades, making them less likely to stay subscribed.

steps.title: Reject illogical verbs
steps.content: Choice B would mean enthusiasm becomes renewed, the opposite of the retention problem. Choice C describes coming together, and Choice D means settling or solving. The verb meaning fade or lessen fits the sentence. The answer is A.
